column2: Row/column orientation pairs use identical underlying data so the structural difference is the only variable.

column3: Row/column oriented table with one set of purely static headers
Row/column oriented table with one set of purely static headers
Row/column hybrid static/dynamic columns along one axis
Row/column hybrid static/dynamic columns along both axes
Multiple hybrid static/dynamic headers along both axes

column2: Single Axis  ·  Row Orientation  ·  Static Headers
Each row = one record. Headers are fixed field names across columns.
→ JSON record:  { employee_id, name, department, salary, hire_date }
Single Axis  ·  Column Orientation  ·  Static Headers
Each column = one record. Headers are fixed field names down the first column.
→ JSON record:  { employee_id, name, department, salary, hire_date }
Single Axis  ·  Row Orientation  ·  Dynamic headers
Headers are value-labels (month names). The header axis has no declared field name — context implies ‘Month'. Values are context-defined (sales)
→ JSON record (after unpivot):  { month, sales }  — 'month'/'sales' are user-supplied field names
Single Axis  ·  Column Orientation  ·  Dynamic headers
Transposed: month names run down the first column

→ JSON record (after unpivot):  { month, sales }  — same record shape as sheet 3
→ Parser config:  orientation=column  |  header_col=1 (dynamic)  |  col_label_row=1 (dynamic)
PIVOT  ·  Row Orientation  ·  Hybrid Static + Dynamic Column Headers
Column headers contain both static headers (account, product) and dynamic headers (year).
PIVOT  ·  Column Orientation  ·  Hybrid Static + Dynamic Column Headers
Transposed: headers run down the first column
CROSSTAB  ·  Both axes  ·  Dynamic × Dynamic headers
Both axes carry value-labels. Neither axis has a declared field name. Corner cell is blank.
→ JSON record (after melt):  { team, severity, tickets }  — all three field names are user-supplied
→ Parser config:  orientation=both  |  row_label_col=1 (dynamic)  |  col_label_row=1 (dynamic)  |  corner=blank
CROSSTAB  ·  Both axes  ·  Static × Static headers
Both axes carry declared field names. Corner cell holds the row-axis field name 'team \ severity'.
→ JSON record (after melt):  { team, severity, tickets }  — same record shape as sheet 7; field names now self-describing
→ Parser config:  orientation=both  |  row_label_col=1 (static)  |  col_label_row=1 (static)  |  corner=row_field_name
Both axes carry multiple header names. Corner cell holds the row-axis field name 'revenue \ company’.
→ JSON record (after melt):  { company, quarter, revenue, currency, scope, industry, hq }

→ Parser config:  orientation=both  |  row_label_col=1 (static)  |  col_label_col=1 (dynamic) |  
     row_label_col=3 (dynamic)  | col_label_col=3 (dynamic) | corner=row_field_name
